description_long: This rock is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a predominantly white surface interspersed with patches of reddish-brown and greenish-gray mineral deposits. The texture appears coarse and uneven, with jagged edges and a rough, granular finish. The white areas suggest the presence of quartz or feldspar, while the reddish-brown zones may indicate iron oxide staining or the presence of other minerals. The greenish-gray streaks add to the visual complexity, possibly hinting at chlorite or serpentine inclusions. The rock's surface is matte and non-reflective, with no visible polish or smoothing from human contact. Its overall form is asymmetrical, making it a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban environment.
description: An irregular, rough-textured rock with white, reddish-brown, and greenish-gray mineral patches. Its jagged, coarse surface and asymmetrical shape make it visually striking.
description_view_0: This view highlights the broad, white surface with prominent reddish-brown and greenish-gray mineral patches, showing the rock's coarse texture.
description_view_1: The rock appears more compact from this angle, with the reddish-brown mineralization dominating and greenish-gray streaks visible along the side.
description_view_2: This side reveals a lighter, mostly white surface with some brownish and grayish mineral bands, and a more angular, blocky profile.
description_view_3: The rock's elongated form is evident here, with a mix of white and greenish-gray minerals and less visible reddish-brown coloration.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside barrier
